role: Minister of Culture and Islamic Guidance (2009-2013); VP for Parliamentary Affairs under Raisi (2021-2024); IRGC veteran
bio:
Mohammad Hosseini is an Iranian politician and [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) veteran who served as Minister of Culture and Islamic Guidance in [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s second cabinet from 2009 to 2013, and later as Vice President for Parliamentary Affairs under [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) from 2021 to 2024.

Hosseini joined the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) following the 1979 revolution and served during the Iran-Iraq War. He holds a PhD in Islamic jurisprudence from Tehran University (1994).

Prior to his ministerial appointment, Hosseini served as head of Sorush Publications, deputy director of [IRIB](/orgs/irib), and advisor to the Islamic Culture and Relations Organization. As head of the [Ministry of Culture](/orgs/ministry-of-culture), he continued the restrictive cultural policies of his predecessor, tightening controls on media, publishing, and the arts.
editorial_notes: Born 1961 in Rafsanjan. IRGC veteran. PhD Islamic jurisprudence Tehran University 1994. Also served as VP Parliamentary Affairs under Raisi 2021-2024.
